chat.author: Slackmoq
chat.text:
englishm: Copying what I just sent to the list in case anyone is looking for Meetecho links:
> Good morning!
> 
> The links for participation in today's hybrid interim sessions are available on this page:
> 
> https://datatracker.ietf.org/meeting/upcoming
> 
> This morning's session link is: https://meetings.conf.meetecho.com/interim/?session=35333
> 
> Please log in to Meetecho even if in person for bluesheet and queuing purposes.
> 
> If using your computer here in the room, please make sure to mute your audio.
> 
> Thanks!
> -Mike

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: Remote participants: if the sound is poor, please say so in here

chat.author: Cullen Jennings
chat.text: +1 to what will said

chat.author: Manish
chat.text: I think the webcam focus is wrong, maybe that's why the video is too blurry.

chat.author: Magnus Westerlund
chat.text: Regarding the video focus it is unclear if we can do anything.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: I can attempt to fix the video focus at the next break

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: Good insight, Mo

chat.author: Martin Duke
chat.text: I guess the reason to get SG > 0 is to allow playback of those SGs in 11b

chat.author: Gwendal Simon
chat.text: For Mo, we would need a "temporary filter", like give me only subgroup 0 for the currentGroup, and then  resume no-filter

chat.author: Slackmoq
chat.text: slackbot: <@U0B9Z06PLBE> has joined the channel

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: +1 Mo again. Priorities as having session-wide relevance worth not losing site of while looking at individual track scenarios

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: *sight

chat.author: Asad Saeed
chat.text: if update_request cancels the previous request, how would you pipeline for vod, so you can keep the wire busy, and not stall when you're done with the current range you requested.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: Maybe as separate fill fetch subscriptions rather than as one that gets updated? Might need to use priorities to get the desired ordering.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: I haven't thought much about using this for VOD yet.

chat.author: Will Law
chat.text: @Asad - your client buffer would cover you and you wouldn;t stall. For example, if you want a 20s buffer with your VOD player, you might start by Subscribig 0-30s. When your buffer drops to 20s, then you request_update for more 10s. Buffer would fill to 30s, then drain to 20s. This sawtooth is typcial VOD player behavior.

chat.author: Asad Saeed
chat.text: For fill_current_group, what priority is this written to on the wire. I think there may be use-cases why you may want the current group to be sent at a higher priority then future subscriptions. Otherwise you might immediately have to stall, or skip forward

chat.author: Asad Saeed
chat.text: current group could be important to lower time to video

chat.author: Asad Saeed
chat.text: s/future subscriptions/future group

chat.author: Gwendal Simon
chat.text: @asad the group order is the tiebreaker. If you have AscendingOrder, then the Objects in the fill stream will be delivered before the live and future Objects https://github.com/afrind/moq-transport/pull/18#issuecomment-4658639034

chat.author: Asad Saeed
chat.text: Probably makes sense to not queue these, pass the error back.

chat.author: Mike English
chat.text: Lunch is here, fwiw

chat.author: Slackmoq
chat.text:
englishm: FYI: I started a new page in the wiki to capture anecdotal reports of interop that various implementations have achieved:
https://github.com/moq-wg/moq-transport/wiki/ad-hoc-interop-reports
Getting formal and systematic interop test results via <#C0B2KQLJGN7> is still the priority, but it'd be helpful for my interop report if people can fill in more info in the wiki page about anything not represented in the interop runner yet.

chat.author: Alessandro Amirante
chat.text: This room is closing soon. Afternoon session's link is https://meetings.conf.meetecho.com/interim/?session=35334
